what_it_does: Defines the per-client RLS layer (gate 2 of a two-gates model) for the claude-design connector in Supabase/Postgres. Creates SQL helper functions (client_id(), is_design_client(), channel_granted()) that read the caller's JWT app_metadata, then declares row-level-security policies on channel_posts, channels, design_submissions, dna_tokens, and design_seeds so a design_client can only insert its own posts/submissions into channels granted to its registry row and read only its granted shared channels plus read-mostly collab context.
observations: client_id() reads app_metadata.client_id from auth.jwt(); is_design_client() checks app_metadata.role == 'design_client'.

channel_granted(chan) is SECURITY DEFINER with a fixed search_path so reading public.clients/public.channels inside a channels policy does not recurse into RLS (would cause infinite recursion / stack-depth).
Explicit ALTER ... security definer and ALTER ... set search_path after CREATE OR REPLACE because replace does not change an existing function's security attribute.
Policies are drop-if-exists then create (idempotent re-apply).
Insert policy ties from_session = client_id() so a client cannot forge another client's posts; submission insert ties submitted_by = client_id().
Closing comment states no new grants are needed because 0001-0005 already granted SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE to authenticated; RLS only gates which rows.
standouts: The recursion-avoidance rationale: a channels policy querying channels must use SECURITY DEFINER to bypass RLS or it recurses infinitely.
The explicit ALTER to FORCE security definer on upgraded environments where CREATE OR REPLACE would silently keep INVOKER.
conventions: Idempotent migration style (drop policy if exists, create or replace function).
Heavy inline commentary explaining the security model and the why, not just the what.
Helper functions marked stable; security-definer functions pin search_path = public.
concerns: client_id()/is_design_client() trust app_metadata claims in the JWT — the security depends entirely on those claims being server-set (this file does not set them; relies on a separate hook).
notes: This is the row-level scoping that ties every design_client read/write to its own registry client_id; the JWT claims it trusts are stamped elsewhere.
questions: What process guarantees app_metadata.client_id/role are server-set and not user-forgeable (this file assumes it)?
How do 0005's boundary policies interact with these design_client policies on the same tables?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header states it is gate 2 (per-client RLS scoping) and the policies/functions implement exactly that.
apparent_themes: Defense in depth (two gates: edge-function posture + RLS at the row)
Registry-is-truth (a client's grants live in its clients row)
Least privilege scoping at the row level
intention_signals: Trying to make Supabase RLS the authoritative per-client boundary so a forwarded design_client JWT can only touch its own rows, independent of (and beneath) the Edge Function's checks.
novelty: The SECURITY DEFINER trick to break RLS-policy self-recursion when a policy must query the very table it guards, plus the explicit re-ALTER to defeat CREATE OR REPLACE not changing the security attribute.
contribution: Contributes the per-client row-level scoping rules and the JWT-claim helper functions for the design connector, including the non-recursive granted-channel lookup pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A Supabase RLS migration scoping the claude-design connector's reads/writes to its own registry client_id via JWT-claim helper functions and per-table policies.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce per-client row-level security as the second gate so a design_client can only access rows belonging to its granted channels and own submissions.
